title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori — Shared Memory R Objects. Uses POSIX shared memory (Linux, macOS) Win32 file mappings (Windows) R’s ALTREP framework let multiple processes machine read physical memory pages. external dependencies. Requires R >= 4.3.0 (ALTLIST). API: share() → ALTREP shared object, map_shared() → open SHM name, shared_name() → extract SHM name, is_shared() → test shared. SHM lifetime automatic — managed R’s garbage collector via chained external pointer finalizers. ALTREP serialization hooks serialize standalone shared objects SHM name (~30 bytes) nested ALTLIST elements (including sub-lists) (parent_name, integer_path), enabling transparent use mirai R serialization path. Nested VECSXP elements zero-copy: level inline self-describing MORL region inside parent SHM.

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Run the full testthat suite devtools::test()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Zero-copy (SHM-backed): atomic vectors (including character vectors arbitrary attributes names, class, levels, dim) data frame columns written directly SHM backed ALTREP consumers. Attributes serialized trailing section SHM region restored via SET_ATTRIB consumer. numeric types, Dataptr_or_null returns SHM pointer reads; Dataptr(writable=TRUE) materializes private copy (COW). character vectors, Elt lazily creates CHARSXP via Rf_mkCharLenCE SHM data; Dataptr_or_null returns NULL force element--element access. Nested lists (zero-copy): VECSXP/LISTSXP elements shared list written inline complete child MORL region data_offset. nested region self-describing (magic bytes + header) recursively contains directory, element data, attributes. Consumers wrap level ALTLIST via lightweight mori_list_view (pointer + bounds + index) rather full mori_shm. Sub-lists therefore stay zero-copy way report is_shared(x) == TRUE; shared_name() returns root SHM name root lists "" sub-lists. Pass-: R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged share(). SHM created.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R exported functions single .Call wrappers. share() calls mori_create dispatches TYPEOF(x): x already mori-backed ALTREP (detected via mori_owned_tag R_altrep_data1(x)), return x unchanged. makes share() idempotent also short-circuits re-sharing sub-list views element vectors extracted ALTLIST. NILSXP → returned -(falls checks). VECSXP/LISTSXP → mori_shm_create_list_call — ALTLIST per-element directory. Writing split two passes: mori_nested_size computes total SHM size (recursing nested VECSXP/LISTSXP elements), mori_nested_write produces complete MORL region target base pointer, recursing child VECSXP/LISTSXP elements calling base + child_data_offset. element independently zero-copy (atomic, without attributes), serialized bytes, nested MORL region. Data frames pairlists go path (pairlists coerced VECSXP via Rf_coerceVector, top level level recursion). STRSXP → mori_shm_create_string_call — ALTSTRING backed SHM offset table + packed string data. Attributes () serialized string data. SHM-eligible types (REALSXP, INTSXP, LGLSXP, RAWSXP, CPLXSXP) → mori_shm_create_vector_call — single ALTREP vector backed SHM. Attributes () serialized vector data. Everything else → returned -(pass-). creation path returns ALTREP result via mori_make_result, chains host extptr (responsible shm_unlink) ALTREP’s external pointer hierarchy. SHM lifetime thus tied R object — ALTREP garbage collected, munmap unlink happen automatically.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: ALTREP classes register Serialized_state Unserialize methods. Standalone shared objects (created share() map_shared()) serialize just SHM name string (~30 bytes). unserialize, mori_Unserialize validates name via mori_is_shm_name() (checks /mori_ prefix POSIX, Local\mori_ Windows), mori_shm_open_and_wrap opens SHM creates fresh ALTREP wrapper. R’s ALTREP serialization framework separately serializes restores object’s attributes. Element vectors sub-lists ALTLIST serialize list(parent_name, int_path) int_path INTSXP length >= 1 giving top-indices root region leaf. Length 1 wire-identical previous scalar (name, index) form, existing serialized blobs remain readable. unserialize, mori_Unserialize validates element types (first element STRSXP, second INTSXP length >= 1) name looks like mori SHM name calling mori_open_path, opens root SHM, walks intermediate VECSXP directory entry via mori_make_list_view (bounds-checking child region parent), finally calls mori_unwrap_element leaf. element’s mori_vec/mori_str struct stores index field (int32_t, -1 standalone, >= 0 ALTLIST elements), mori_list_view likewise stores index (-1 root view, >= 0 sub-list) — indices mori_build_path collects walking keeper chain view extptrs serialize time. Fallback full materialization : - COW-materialized vectors (data2 set) — returns materialized copy - Nesting depth exceeds MORI_MAX_PATH (64) mori_build_path

title: CLAUDE.md
text: first 4 bytes SHM region identify layout: - 0x4D4F524C (“MORL”): ALTLIST — contains element directory + per-element data - 0x4D4F5248 (“MORH”): ALTREP vector — 64-byte header + data + optional serialized attributes - 0x4D4F5253 (“MORS”): ALTSTRING — 24-byte header + offset table + packed strings + optional serialized attributes

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Atomic vector (MORH): 64-byte header, data starts byte 64 (64-byte aligned SIMD). Serialized attributes () follow vector data. attributes (names, dim, class, levels, tzone, etc.) serialized pairlist trailing attrs section. consumer, mori_restore_attrs unserializes sets via SET_ATTRIB. ALTLIST (MORL): Header + element directory + per-element data regions. element directory entry (32 bytes): data_offset(8) + data_size(8) + sexptype(4) + attrs_size(4) + length(8). sexptype != 0 means zero-copy (raw data, string, nested list); sexptype == 0 means serialized bytes. sexptype == STRSXP indicates string element whose data region contains offset table + packed strings. sexptype == VECSXP indicates nested MORL region inline data_offset size data_size (child’s header, directory, elements, attrs live inside region); parent directory entry’s attrs_size always 0 VECSXP children — attributes part child region’s trailing attrs. non-nested zero-copy entries attrs_size > 0, element’s serialized attributes appended raw data within data region (offset data_offset + data_size - attrs_size). consumer, mori_restore_attrs unserializes applies . String vector (MORS): 24-byte header + offset table + packed string data + optional serialized attributes. offset table entry (16 bytes): str_offset(int64) + str_length(int32) + str_encoding(int32). str_offset relative start packed string area. str_length < 0 means NA_STRING. str_encoding cetype_t value (0=native, 1=UTF-8, 2=Latin-1, 3=bytes). layout used string elements within ALTLIST regions (offset table starts element’s data_offset; element-level attrs use directory entry’s attrs_size field).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori_tag (C global, altrep.c): Interned symbol Rf_install("mori"). Role: ALTLIST cache sentinel (VECSXP element marker, never extptr tag) — distinguishes “yet accessed” cached NULL element. mori_shm_tag (C global, altrep.c): Interned symbol Rf_install("mori_shm"). Tag daemon-side SHM mapping extptrs; addr mori_shm *; finalizer calls munmap. mori_host_tag (C global, altrep.c): Interned symbol Rf_install("mori_host"). Tag host-unlink extptrs (created mori_make_result); addr mori_shm *; finalizer calls shm_unlink/CloseHandle. Sits shm terminus keeper chain. mori_owned_tag (C global, altrep.c): Interned symbol Rf_install("mori_owned"). Tag every mori ALTREP’s data1 extptr (view, vec, str — root / sub-list / standalone / element). addr type recovered TYPEOF(x) ALTREP boundary: VECSXP → mori_list_view *, STRSXP → mori_str *, else mori_vec *. Standalone-vs-element carried struct’s index field (-1 standalone/root, >= 0 element/sub-list) redundantly recoverable tag data1.prot (shm_tag → standalone, owned_tag → element/sub-list). is_shared() single pointer comparison tag; mori_shm_name() adds one prot hop returns name prot’s tag shm_tag.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: SHM lifetime fully automatic, managed chaining host extptr (responsible shm_unlink/CloseHandle) ALTREP object’s external pointer hierarchy. Host side (share): mori_shm_create allocates SHM region mmaps ; POSIX, fd closed immediately mmap (mapping stays valid; mori_shm struct retain fd). mori_make_result splits ownership: ALTREP wrapper gets mapping (addr, size) via daemon-side SHM extptr (tag mori_shm_tag, finalizer mori_shm_finalizer → munmap), host extptr gets handle (tag mori_host_tag, addr mori_shm * holding name POSIX unlink, HANDLE Windows) via mori_host_finalizer. host extptr chained protected value ALTREP’s SHM extptr. ALTREP garbage collected, finalizers run: munmap + unlink. R_RegisterCFinalizerEx(..., TRUE) ensures cleanup session exit. Consumer side (map_shared/unserialize): mori_shm_open maps region read-(size discovered via fstat/VirtualQuery); POSIX, fd closed immediately mmap. shm extptr (tag mori_shm_tag) holds mori_shm * mori_shm_finalizer calls munmap (never unlink). lists, shm extptr’s immediate child root view extptr (tag mori_owned_tag, addr mori_list_view *, index=-1) becomes ALTREP data1; sub-list views (index >= 0) chain parent view extptr way. ALTLIST element vectors hold mori_vec mori_str extptr (also tagged mori_owned_tag) whose protected value references immediate parent view extptr (shm extptr, vec/str root MORH/MORS regions). SHM name stored shm->name: shared_name() reads via data1.prot → shm_ptr.addr->name data1.prot’s tag mori_shm_tag, returns "" otherwise (element / sub-list). Element lifetime: Element vectors sub-lists extracted ALTLIST keep root SHM alive keeper chain (leaf element → parent view(s) → root shm extptr → host extptr). Every hop chain carries one mori_owned_tag (views/vec/str leaves), mori_shm_tag (terminus), mori_host_tag (terminus, host side ). Even enclosing ALTLIST(s) garbage collected, every R_MakeExternalPtr(addr, tag, prot) keeps prot slot alive, chain survives long descendant referenced. mori_build_path keeper-chain walker: walks mori_owned_tag hops (always mori_list_view * since vec/str extptrs ever leaves), collecting view indices (skipping root view’s -1) reaches shm extptr reads name.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori.h: Types (mori_shm, mori_buf, mori_vec index field, mori_list_view), function declarations, MORI_ALIGN64 macro shm.c: Platform-abstracted SHM create/open/close. Linux, uses open("/dev/shm/...") directly avoid -lrt link dependency. macOS, uses shm_open/shm_unlink (libc). Finalizers host daemon side. serialize.c: Counting pass (mori_serialize_count), fixed-buffer write (mori_serialize_into), unserialize--buffer (mori_unserialize_from), mori_sizeof_elt altrep.c: ALTREP class definitions methods, mori_make_vector/mori_make_string/mori_make_list_view helpers, mori_unwrap_element helper (shared element extraction mori_list_Elt mori_open_path; validates dir bounds enclosing region), mori_restore_attrs helper, mori_is_shm_name validator, unified creation dispatcher (mori_create), recursive writer helpers mori_nested_size / mori_nested_write, consumer-side open+wrap dispatch (mori_shm_open_and_wrap), path open (mori_open_path), identity check (mori_is_shared), name extraction (mori_shm_name), serialization hooks (Serialized_state/Unserialize), mori_build_path (keeper-chain walker), mori_altrep_init init.c: R_init_mori, .Call registration table (4 entry points: mori_create, mori_shm_open_and_wrap, mori_is_shared, mori_shm_name) .Call names match C function names. entry points take single SEXP argument.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: mori-package.R: Package docs share.R: four exported functions single .Call wrappers — dispatch error handling C level. share() → mori_create, map_shared() → mori_shm_open_and_wrap, shared_name() → mori_shm_name, is_shared() → mori_is_shared.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Uses testthat (edition 3). entry point tests/testthat.R; individual tests live tests/testthat/test-*.R, grouped topic: test-passthrough.R — pass-(formulas, NULL, closures/environments inside lists) test-vectors.R — atomic vector round-trip (double, integer, logical, raw, complex, matrix, empty) test-strings.R — ALTSTRING behaviour (basic, NA, empty, UTF-8, large, data frame column, list element, Dataptr materialization via make.unique/duplicated) test-list.R — ALTLIST (data frames, mixed lists, NULL elements, duplicate, pairlist) test-nested.R — nested VECSXP elements (sub-lists, list-columns, deep structures, path-based serialization, sub-list COW, GC nested references) test-attributes.R — attribute preservation (names, factor, Date, POSIXct, matrix) test-cow.R — copy--write numeric string vectors, Dataptr_or_null materialized vec test-serialization.R — ALTREP Serialized_state/Unserialize hooks (standalone, element, compact size, COW fallback, attributes) test-gc.R — automatic SHM cleanup garbage collector test-api.R — share()/map_shared()/shared_name()/is_shared() behaviour including invalid inputs tests run unconditionally; nothing gated behind skip_on_cran environment variables.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Linux: SHM lives /dev/shm/ (tmpfs). Accessed via open()/unlink() directly, avoiding -lrt link dependency shm_open requires. MAP_POPULATE pre-faults pages. macOS: Kernel-backed SHM via shm_open/shm_unlink (libc, extra link flags). MAP_POPULATE -op (defined 0). Windows: Page-file-backed via CreateFileMappingA/MapViewOfFile. kernel32 always available. Host must keep mapping handle alive consumers opened (GC-chained host extptr handles automatically).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Gao C (2026). mori: Shared Memory R Objects. R package version 0.1.0.9000, https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mori: Shared Memory for R Objects},   author = {Charlie Gao},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0.9000},   url = {https://shikokuchuo.net/mori/}, }

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared Memory R Objects → share() writes R object shared memory returns shared version → ALTREP serialization hooks — shared objects serialize compactly work transparently serialize() mirai() → ALTREP-backed lazy access — 100-column data frame one mmap; columns materialize first touch → OS-level shared memory (POSIX / Win32) — pure C, external dependencies; read-processes, preventing corruption shared data → Automatic cleanup — shared memory freed R object garbage collected

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: share() writes R object shared memory returns zero-copy ALTREP view. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks, working transparently mirai R serialization path. Shared memory automatically freed object garbage collected.
code: library(mori)  # Share a vector — returns an ALTREP-backed object x <- share(rnorm(1e6)) mean(x) #> [1] 0.0005982035  # Serialized form is ~100 bytes, not ~8 MB x |> serialize(NULL) |> length() #> [1] 124

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: shared_name() extracts SHM name shared object. map_shared() opens shared region name — useful accessing data another process without serialization:
code: x <- share(1:1e6)  # Extract the SHM name nm <- shared_name(x) nm #> [1] "/mori_4c0f_1"  # Another process can map the same region by name y <- map_shared(nm) identical(x[], y[]) #> [1] TRUE

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared objects can sent local daemons — ALTREP serialization hooks ensure SHM name crosses wire, daemon maps physical memory. Elements shared list also serialize compactly — element travels reference position parent shared region, full data:
code: library(lobstr) library(mirai)  daemons(1)  x <- share(rnorm(1e6))  # Worker maps the same shared memory — 0 bytes copied m <- mirai(list(mean = mean(x), size = lobstr::obj_size(x)), x = x) m[] #> $mean #> [1] 0.0008675476 #>  #> $size #> 840 B  daemons(0) daemons(3)  # Share a list — all 3 vectors in a single shared region x <- list(a = rnorm(1e6), b = rnorm(1e6), c = rnorm(1e6)) |> share()  # Each element is sent as (parent_name, index) — zero-copy on the worker mirai_map(x, \(v) lobstr::obj_size(v) |> format())[.flat] #>       a       b       c  #> "840 B" "840 B" "840 B"  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Parallel computing multiplies memory. 8 workers need 210 MB dataset, 1.7 GB serialization, transfer, deserialization — 8 separate copies consuming RAM. mori eliminates . share() writes data shared memory . worker maps physical pages, receiving reference ~300 bytes instead full dataset — payload ~700,000 times smaller, translates significant saving memory usage well total runtime:
code: daemons(8)  # 200 MB data frame — 5 columns × 5M rows df <- as.data.frame(matrix(rnorm(25e6), ncol = 5)) shared_df <- share(df)  boot_mean <- \(i, data) colMeans(data[sample(nrow(data), replace = TRUE), ])  # Without mori — each daemon deserializes a full copy mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, data = df)[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   2.135  38.222   5.823  # With mori — each daemon maps the same shared memory mirai_map(1:8, boot_mean, data = shared_df)[] |> system.time() #>    user  system elapsed  #>   1.377  27.121   3.949  daemons(0)

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: atomic vector types lists / data frames written directly shared memory, attributes preserved end--end. Pairlists coerced lists. share() returns ALTREP wrappers point shared pages — deserialization, per-process memory allocation. R objects (environments, closures, language objects) returned unchanged share() — shared memory region created. Diagram showing share() writing object OS-backed shared memory, memory-mapped processes using zero-copy ALTREP wrappers

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: data frame 10 columns lives single shared region. task touches 3 columns pays 3. Character strings accessed lazily per element.

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared memory managed R’s garbage collector. SHM region stays alive long shared object (element extracted ) referenced R. references remain, garbage collector frees shared memory automatically. Important: Always assign result share() variable. shared memory kept alive R object reference — result used temporarily (assigned), garbage collector may free shared memory consumer process mapped .

title: Shared Memory for R Objects
text: Shared data mapped read-. Mutations always local — R’s copy--write mechanism ensures processes continue reading original shared data: Structural changes list data frame (adding, removing, reordering elements) produce regular R list. shared region unaffected. Modifying values within shared vector (e.g., X[1] <- 0) materializes just vector private copy. vectors shared region stay zero-copy. – Please note mori project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

what: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
title: mori: Shared Memory for R Objects — mori-package
text: Share R objects via shared memory share(), access processes map_shared(), using R's ALTREP framework zero-copy memory-mapped access. Shared objects serialize compactly via ALTREP serialization hooks. Shared memory automatically freed R object garbage collected.

title: Create a Shared Object — share
text: atomic vectors (including character vectors attributes names, dim, class, levels) lists data frames whose elements vectors, ALTREP-backed object reads directly shared memory. object (environments, closures, language objects, NULL), input returned unchanged shared memory region created.

title: Create a Shared Object — share
text: Attributes stored alongside data shared memory region restored consumer side. Character vectors use packed layout elements materialised lazily access. serialised (e.g. serialize() across mirai() call), shared object represented compactly SHM name (~30 bytes) rather contents. shared memory region managed automatically. stays alive long returned object (element extracted ) referenced R, freed garbage collector references remain. share() idempotent: calling object already backed shared memory returns input unchanged without allocating new region. Important: always assign result share() variable. shared memory kept alive R object reference — result used temporary (assigned), garbage collector may free shared memory consumer process mapped .
